'''''Sigil''''' was a type of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] used by the [[Lok Revenants/Legends|Lok Revenants]].

==Characteristics==
The ''Sigil'' were a class of single-seat [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] with two engines. ''Sigil''s were equipped with [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], and [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]].

==History==
The [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Nym/Legends|Nym]] acquired several of these for his band. They were used to raid [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] shipping around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. After Nym [[Battle of Lok (32 BBY)|was driven from his base]], his forces continued to [[Occupation of Karthakk|fight]] against the Trade Federation occupation of their [[Star system/Legends|system]] and continued to use the ''Sigil''. [[Sol Sixxa]], the leader of the [[Mere Resistance]], used one as his personal starfighter.

==Behind the scenes==
A starfighter of this class appears as a non-player character wingmate in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] and again in its sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] {{1st}}
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]''
